learning_outcomes.description: Analyse a brief or specification for an interactive technology, software, or IT application and apply your expertise to selecting appropriate combinations of tools, technologies and techniques to design and create a prototype solution.

Apply teamworking and project management techniques to collaborating effectively in both the scoping and creation of a substantial prototype that meets the goals of the brief or specification and is delivered on time.
Apply human computer interaction design methods to design and critically evaluate the usability and features of your team’s prototype application.
Apply effective communication skills to justify the need, design, and capabilities of your team’s prototype application to others.

Research and critique a range of competing applications and analyse their strengths and weaknesses to compare these against your team's solution [THIS LEARNING OBJECTIVE CONTRIBUTES TO AQF9 RESEARCH FOR MASTERS PROGRAMS]

Critically reflect on the ethical, security and data privacy decisions made in the design and implementation of your team’s project and analyse the impact of these decisions on the prototype solution.

Appraise (reflect) on your own experience of working in a team to create prototype application and propose improvements to the process, and your own approach, that you could apply in your future professional practice.

assessments.due_date:
Assessed contribution reviews are conducted during selected scheduled Studio sessions between Week 3 and Week 11. 13/10/2026 2:00 pm

Final Individual Contribution Verification Demo 19/10/2026 10:00 am
assessments.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Practical/ Demonstration, Product/ Design, Reflection
assessments.task_description:
Individual Project Contributions are assessed through a sequence of linked assessment activities conducted throughout the semester following team formation. These activities are designed to assess each student's individual contribution to the team project, professional engagement, and ability to explain and justify their work.

This assessment consists of:

  1. Contribution Reviews (Pass/Fail)
  2. Students are assessed on their ongoing contribution to the team project through a series of contribution reviews conducted during selected scheduled studio sessions between Week 3 and Week 11.
  3. A total of 7 Contribution Reviews will be conducted. Students are required to satisfactorily complete at least 5 of these reviews to satisfy the minimum hurdle requirement.
  4. Final Individual Contribution Verification Demo (30%) (Week 12)
  5. Conducted during a scheduled studio session as an identity-verified and secure assessment. In accordance with UQ Assessment Policy, your presentation will be recorded.


To provide feedback before Census, students will receive early formative feedback through scheduled studio activities and the Contribution Review process.

Students should refer to the assessment task sheet and semester plan for detailed requirements, assessment criteria, assessment schedules, intervention and moderation process and evidence expectations. Students are responsible for reading and following those requirements.

Use of AI/MT

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students are responsible for following the submission, participation, and evidence requirements described in this task sheet.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

Team Project Checkpoint 1 (Formative - Pass/Fail) 31/08/2026 10:00 am

Team Project Checkpoint 2 (Formative - Pass/Fail) 9/10/2026 5:00 pm

Final Project Submission (Final Team Codebase, Prototype, Project Artefacts and Team Report) 23/10/2026 1:00 pm
assessments.category: Computer Code, Essay/ Critique, Practical/ Demonstration, Product/ Design, Project
assessments.task_description:
The Team Project is the primary learning activity in this course. Working in interdisciplinary teams, students design, develop, evaluate, and deliver a substantial software or digital technology prototype in response to a project brief. Teams are expected to work in a practice-based, industry-style project environment, apply appropriate technical and design methods, manage project scope and risk, and demonstrate iterative progress throughout the semester.

This assessment is weighted at 45% of the course, with the following breakdown:

  1. Statement of Work and Team Pitch (10%)
  2. Team Project Checkpoint 1 (Formative - Pass/Fail)
  3. Team Project Checkpoint 2 (Formative - Pass/Fail)
  4. Final Team Codebase, Prototype, and Project Artefacts (25%)
  5. Final Team Report (10%)

If a team makes no final project submission, its most recent checkpoint submission may be assessed against the final prototype, codebase and project-artefact rubric. This fallback may be used to determine an assessment result, but it does not automatically satisfy the requirement to submit the final project outcome or the Team Project hurdle.

Teams are expected to maintain project artefacts, code, documentation, design materials, and project management records in appropriate shared systems throughout the semester as specified in the task sheet. Version control and project management records may be used as evidence of iterative development, team progress, and individual contribution. Detailed submission requirements, file formats, artefact expectations, and assessment criteria will be provided in the assessment task sheet. 

Students should refer to the assessment task sheet and semester plan for detailed requirements, assessment criteria, assessment schedules, intervention and moderation process and evidence expectations. Students are responsible for reading and following those requirements.

In accordance with UQ Assessment Policy, your presentation may be recorded.

Use of AI

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Team Project Sequence submissions are as follows:

assessments.task_description:
This assessment requires students to critically reflect on their experience of contributing to a team-based capstone project and to demonstrate evidence of their learning, professional development, decision-making, and individual contribution.

Students should refer to the assessment task sheet and semester plan for detailed requirements, assessment criteria, assessment schedules, and evidence expectations.

Use of AI/MT

AI tools may be used to support learning and task completion in this course, and the course provides guidance on appropriate use. Students are expected to apply judgement when using these tools, to check outputs carefully, and to ensure that all submitted work reflects their own understanding. Students are responsible for ensuring that any use of AI complies with academic integrity requirements and is appropriately acknowledged. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission must be via Turnitin through Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Studio & consultation

Students will work with support from course teaching staff on their team projects and related activities. Students are expected to work on their projects during and outside of this weekly scheduled activity. An exception to the duration of this activity is in week 13 when the finish time is 20:00 to allow sufficient time for the course Exhibit.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07

learning_activities.topic:
Lectures

During the first hour of each studio session, there will be important course information, or Industry guest lectures, or assessed class-based activities. Topics may include working together, project management, industry best practices and related topics. Dates for information sessions, guest lectures and course-wide activities will be advertised in the lecture schedule.
